page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-44 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 44 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 22 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 352.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for integer and vector operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark data shows strong performance in specific workloads. The instance achieves a Passmark CPU score of 47,633.9 and an encryption score of 38,870.97, both placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 41.56 GB/sec, alongside strong image processing capabilities. However, multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and database operations fall within the average range. Large block memory bandwidth is measured at 112.56 GB/sec for read operations and 49.98 GB/sec for write operations.

While the instance demonstrates strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium language models, text generation for large 70B models is poor, yielding only 2.41 tokens per second due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration. This highlights a clear tradeoff when using CPU-only resources for large-scale AI generation. Given its high memory density and strong encryption and web serving metrics, this server is suited for memory-intensive database operations, web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-44 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 44 dedicated vCPUs and 352.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers strong performance in static web serving at 41.56 GB/sec, image processing, and CPU encryption, ranking in the top 25% for these benchmarks. However, database operations and multi-core CPU performance remain in the average tier. A notable tradeoff is its poor text generation speed of 2.41 tokens per second for large 70B language models, reflecting the limitations of CPU-only execution for heavy AI workloads. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-44 is a memory-optimized server with 44 vCPUs and 352 GB RAM. It features strong encryption and web serving performance but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-44 is a memory-optimized server featuring 44 vCPUs, 352 GB RAM, and strong web serving and encryption performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high memory density, and strong cryptographic and web serving capabilities.
bullet_points: 44 dedicated vCPUs and 352.0 GB of RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8481C
Strong performance in static web serving and CPU encryption tasks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support for vector operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in this configuration
Poor text generation performance for large-scale 70B language models
Suited for memory-intensive databases, web serving, and software compilation
